title: View Cell QR Code And Identify Station Details From Cell Selection
summary: Use the cell selection view in the OptiSweep map interface to select a map cell, read the displayed 8-digit cell code (also described as the QR code or node code), and determine whether the selected cell is a station by checking whether it is an orange cell and whether workstation ID information is shown.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when support personnel need to identify a selected map cell's unique 8-digit cell code and determine whether the selected cell is a station with workstation ID information shown in the cell details view.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret design-oriented map detail fields as primary troubleshooting indicators when the source says support should focus mainly on node code and workstation ID.
Do not assume a cell has multiple cell code values; the source describes the cell code as unique.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the cell selection view to identify the selected cell's node code/QR code and determine whether workstation ID information is present for station cells.

safety_notes: This source segment is instructional/reference-oriented and does not describe a control action on equipment.
Support should focus primarily on the node code/cell code and workstation ID fields called out in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the cell selection view in the map interface
Visual access to the selected cell information panel

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep map interface with cell selection available
system_preconditions: Cell selection is selected in the map interface
A map cell can be clicked to display cell information

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing cell selection details, including QR code/node code display and station workstation ID information for orange cells.

steps.title: Open or use cell selection view
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct map object selection mode is active so cell details can be viewed.
steps.instruction: Open the map interface and use the cell selection view so that cell information can be displayed when a map cell is selected.
steps.expected_result: Cell selection is active and the interface is ready for a cell click.
steps.healthy_condition: The map is in cell selection mode and a selected cell information panel is available.
steps.failure_condition: Cell selection is not active or the interface does not present cell information for selected cells.

actions.label: Use cell selection
actions.exact_action: Use the cell selection view in the map interface.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cell selection screen/frame and the selected cell information panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: The map interface does not allow cell selection.
The selected cell information panel is not visible after entering cell selection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When cell selection is selected, you can click on one of the cells to view the QR code.
Training slide explaining how cell selection displays a cell's QR code/node code and, for station cells, the workstation ID.

steps.title: Select a map cell
steps.purpose: Display the information associated with a specific map location.
steps.instruction: Click on a cell in the map to display its cell information.
steps.expected_result: The selected cell's information appears in the cell details area.
steps.healthy_condition: A cell click causes the interface to show details for that location.
steps.failure_condition: Clicking a cell does not display cell information or the selected location cannot be identified.

actions.label: Click map cell
actions.exact_action: Click on a cell in the map to display its cell information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected cell and the details panel that appears after clicking it.

stop_or_escalate_if: Cell information does not appear after selecting a cell.
The selected cell cannot be matched to a displayed details panel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When cell selection is selected, you can click on one of the cells to view the QR code.
Cell selection view lets the user click a map cell to see cell information including the QR code, node code, or 8-digit cell code for that location.

steps.title: Read the 8-digit cell code
steps.purpose: Identify the unique location code shown for the selected cell.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed 8-digit number and treat it as the cell code for that location. The source also refers to this value as the QR code or node code.
steps.expected_result: A single 8-digit cell code is identified for the selected location.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed value is a unique 8-digit identifier for the selected cell.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed value is missing, unclear, or interpreted as multiple values when the source says the cell code is unique.

actions.label: Read cell code
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed 8-digit number and treat it as the cell code for that location, also referred to in the source as the QR code or node code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The QR code/node code field showing the 8-digit cell code.

stop_or_escalate_if: No 8-digit code is shown for the selected cell.
There is ambiguity about the code value for the selected location.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When cell selection is selected, you can click on one of the cells to view the QR code; the 8-digit number is the cell code of that location.
You are not going to have multiple values for a cell code; it is a unique cell code and a physical QR code number.

steps.title: Determine whether the cell is a station
steps.purpose: Identify whether the selected cell represents a station based on the map color convention described in the source.
steps.instruction: Check whether the selected cell is an orange cell, because the source states orange cells are stations.
steps.expected_result: The user determines whether the selected cell is a station.
steps.healthy_condition: An orange selected cell is recognized as a station cell.
steps.failure_condition: The station status cannot be determined from the selected cell or the color indication is not visible.

actions.label: Check cell color
actions.exact_action: Check whether the selected cell is an orange cell, because the source states orange cells are stations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Orange station cells on the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cell color is not visible or cannot be interpreted.
The interface does not clearly indicate whether the selected cell is orange.

steps.title: Check for workstation ID on station cells
steps.purpose: Capture the additional station-specific detail shown when the selected cell is a station.
steps.instruction: If the selected cell is a station cell, look for the additional workstation ID information shown in the cell details panel.
steps.expected_result: For a station cell, workstation ID information is identified in the details panel.
steps.healthy_condition: A selected orange station cell shows additional information including workstation ID.
steps.failure_condition: A station cell does not show the expected workstation ID information or the details panel is incomplete.

actions.label: Inspect workstation ID
actions.exact_action: If the selected cell is a station cell, look for the additional workstation ID information shown in the cell details panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The station cell details panel showing additional information including workstation ID.

stop_or_escalate_if: An orange station cell does not show workstation ID information.
The details panel appears inconsistent with the selected station cell.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you select an orange cell it shows additional information including the workstation ID.
Selecting an orange station cell shows extra information, including the workstation ID or station ID.

steps.title: Use the cell code when communicating the destination
steps.purpose: Apply the identified location code in support communication.
steps.instruction: Use the displayed cell code when communicating a destination cell to an operator, as described in the training example.
steps.expected_result: The destination location is communicated using the displayed unique cell code.
steps.healthy_condition: The communicated destination uses the selected cell's displayed unique code.
steps.failure_condition: The destination is communicated without the correct displayed cell code or with ambiguous location information.

actions.label: Reference cell code for communication
actions.exact_action: Use the displayed cell code when communicating a destination cell to an operator, as described in the training example.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The displayed node code/QR code used as the location identifier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed cell code cannot be read clearly enough to communicate.
There is uncertainty about whether the displayed code matches the intended destination cell.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The training segment describes using the displayed cell code as the location identifier in support discussion.
The cell code shown in cell selection is a unique identifier for the location and represents the physical QR code number.

success_criteria: The selected map cell's 8-digit cell code is identified from the cell details view.
The user can determine whether the selected cell is a station based on the orange cell indication.
If the selected cell is a station, the workstation ID information is identified.
healthy_conditions: Cell selection mode allows a map cell to be clicked and inspected.
The selected cell displays a single 8-digit QR code/node code/cell code.
Orange cells are recognized as stations and show additional workstation ID information when selected.
failure_conditions: Cell selection does not display cell information.
The 8-digit cell code is not visible or is ambiguous.
Station status cannot be determined from the selected cell.
A selected orange station cell does not show the expected workstation ID information.
Support personnel focus on non-primary design-oriented fields instead of the node code and workstation ID emphasized by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for SME review if the selected cell does not show the expected QR code/node code information.
Escalate for SME review if an orange station cell does not show workstation ID information.
Escalate if map details appear inconsistent and the issue depends on design-oriented fields the source says are not normally the support focus.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cell selection view, displayed QR code/node code, orange station cells, and workstation ID details.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When cell selection is selected, you can click on one of the cells to view the QR code; the 8-digit number is the cell code of that location.
Orange cells represent stations; if you select an orange cell it shows additional information including the workstation ID.

missing_details: The source does not provide a precise navigation path for opening the cell selection view.
The source does not provide a formal escalation owner or team.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this reference task.
The source does not enumerate all fields shown in the cell details panel beyond emphasizing node code and workstation ID as the main support-relevant items.
